question: Authoring surface residence: a new bench author verb with its own harness/author subsystem and server, or mutating routes added to the operator bench serve?
author: claude/design

question: Spec byte fidelity model: the wizard emits YAML into an editable text pane whose exact bytes are previewed and locked, or a structured form model that serializes at lock time?
author: claude/design

question: Stage execution from the authoring surface: excluded in v1 (run/grade/judge stay CLI; the observer watches), or include a run trigger with process management?
author: claude/design

question: Draft residence: plain directories under the workspace root (no ledger yet, so invisible to the operator home until locked), or an explicit drafts registry?
author: claude/design

rationale: Recommended option accepted via 2026-07-04 session directive ('Begin implementing in the sequence you recommend'). A separate bench author verb and harness/author subsystem keeps the operator observer contractually GET-only (EVAL-14 AC-8 untouched) and gives the mutating surface its own enumerable route table.
author: jyang

rationale: Recommended option accepted via 2026-07-04 session directive. The lock hashes exact file bytes; only a text-pane-as-canonical model guarantees the sha the user saw is the sha that ledgered — the wizard is a one-way generator into the pane, never a parallel source of truth.
author: jyang

rationale: Recommended option accepted via 2026-07-04 session directive. Stage execution is long-lived process management with its own obligations; the authoring surface stays one ledgered operation (the lock) and the observer already watches runs.
author: jyang

rationale: Recommended option accepted via 2026-07-04 session directive. A draft is a directory without a ledger — exactly what the workspace scan already tolerates silently; the first ledger event (the lock) is what makes it an experiment.
author: jyang
